raw_consumer_statement:
My credit card was stolen on XX/XX/year> at a XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX, XXXX, by a man who was in line behind me. He charged {$160.00} on my Barclays XXXX XXXX credit card at the XXXX XXXX, at XXXX a.m. and immediately went to XXXX on XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX, XXXX, and used the card for {$660.00}, at XXXX a.m. I reported the card as stolen within XXXX hours to the Credit Card company. They said they would investigate and immediately return the misappropriated funds. They initially credited our account, but later rescinded. Within a few days, Barclays debited my account for more than a XXXX dollars, because they refunded XXXX XXXX XXXX times the amount stolen ( XXXX x XXXX {$640.00} ) and the entire amount ( {$660.00} ) to XXXX XXXX Their reasoning was they claimed that I was still in possession of the card, thus accusing me of fraud. I made very clear to them that I was not in possession of the card when these charges were made. 

I filed a complaint to the XXXX XXXX Police ( XXXX XXXX XXXX, case # XXXX ) who are still investigating using photos from and information given to me by XXXX XXXX management. They are also working with XXXX. I have called the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, numerous times, explaining our injury. It has been almost two months, and we are still awaiting remediation. The first investigation they completed denied our claim, saying that we were in possession of the card at the time the charges were made, which is false. They have reopened the investigation, asking us for more documentation ( we supplied the police report ). We called them again last XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, to check on the investigation, and try to expedite it. They asked us to fax them the police report, which we did the same day. I asked them to get a XXXX involved, or do something that would hurry this along. They said they would call me back within XXXX hours, which they didn't. 

Representatives from XXXX 's have repeatedly suggested that I was the one committing fraud, not the person who stole my card.
